name: search-filter
description: Small, deterministic fixture exercising the proposed/belief/scope search-filter axes and the derived-memory-twin belief-inheritance behavior across the scored (searchDatabase) and enumerate (enumerateEntries) search paths.
purpose: Chunk-0b WI-0b.5 filter-behavior + whyMatched + rank-metric goldens (docs/design/execution/chunk-0b/anchors.md Section D.1/D.2/D.3). Every 'primary' asset's body/description/tags include the shared token "gridlock" so a single FTS query ("gridlock") surfaces the whole primary corpus on the scored path, while an empty query surfaces it on the enumerate path -- the same logical entry set observed through both code paths for an apples-to-apples comparison. One deliberate exception (silent-twin-base.derived.md) does NOT carry the gridlock token -- see its notes entry below.
notes: CORRECTED FINDING (WI-0b.5a, verified empirically against HEAD cd25a769443f4dbe566a418316b67e843ba1dbb9 -- see the ledger): docs/design/execution/chunk-0b/anchors.md Section D.1 characterizes the two-path difference as 'scored path lacks this call [inheritDerivedTwinBeliefStates]'. Direct testing shows this is imprecise: searchDatabase DOES call inheritDerivedTwinBeliefStates (src/indexer/search/db-search.ts:459, before ranking), and because its later filter chain (:513-530) derives from the SAME mutated array via chained .filter() calls (which preserve object references), the scored path's own belief filter sees the inherited state too. For a twin that IS an FTS/vector candidate for the query (memories/base-memory.derived.md, tagged 'gridlock' like its base), BOTH paths agree on filter membership under every belief mode -- verified by this fixture's 'base-memory' pair, which is a PARITY case, not a divergence case.

THE REAL ASYMMETRY (memories/silent-twin-base.md + memories/silent-twin-base.derived.md): the true two-path divergence is candidate-POOL membership, not filter code. searchDatabase's candidate pool comes from searchFts/vector matches for the query's own tokens (FTS indexes description/tags/searchHints/aliases -- NOT raw markdown body prose, unless index.indexBodyOpening is enabled, off by default); enumerateEntries's candidate pool is getAllEntries for the type, entirely independent of query text. silent-twin-base.derived.md deliberately carries NO 'gridlock' token anywhere in its indexed fields (it uses 'duskfall' instead), so a gridlock-query scored search NEVER includes it (zero FTS relevance -- not a candidate, so inheritDerivedTwinBeliefStates never even sees it), under any belief filter. The SAME twin, reached via an empty-query enumerate, is ALWAYS a candidate and therefore reliably inherits silent-twin-base's 'contradicted' belief state, appearing under belief=all/historical and disappearing under belief=current. This is the reviewed-diff-worthy divergence Chunk 5's filter-path unification must account for.

memories/active-memory.md (beliefState: active, quality: curated, scope_user: alice) is the WI-0b.5b whyMatched representative: rich tags/searchHints/aliases/description all share the 'gridlock' token so a 'gridlock' query fires nearly every buildWhyMatched branch (name-token/tags/searchHints/aliases/description matches, curated-metadata boost, metadata-confidence boost, active belief-state reason) on one hit.

memories/asserted-memory.md (scope_agent), memories/superseded-memory.md (scope_run), memories/deprecated-memory.md (scope_channel) each pin one entryMatchesScope key so scope filtering is exercised across all four StashEntryScope keys (the fifth, scope_user, is covered by active-memory + proposed-memory + knowledge/contradicted-knowledge.md).

memories/proposed-memory.md (quality: proposed) pins isProposedQuality's default-exclude / --include-proposed behavior, identical on both search paths (not part of the asymmetry -- both paths call isProposedQuality the same way, and it never touches the candidate-pool-construction difference described above).
memories/plain-memory.md carries no quality/beliefState/scope frontmatter at all -- the neutral baseline present in every gridlock-query filter combination's result set.

knowledge/contradicted-knowledge.md (beliefState: contradicted, type knowledge, scope_user: bob) proves matchBeliefFilter applies beyond the memory type (03's belief-filter widening) and that inheritDerivedTwinBeliefStates only ever touches type==='memory' entries (this one is never eligible for inheritance).
